title: Linking to a single decision
question: Should the viewer let you link to one specific decision, or only ever open to its default?
rationale: Carry the selected decision's id in the URL hash (...#d5): clicking updates it, opening the link reopens that decision, and back/forward step through selections. Chosen so a shared link lands on the decision in question while staying client-side, keeping the offline single-file design that server routes would break.

tradeoffs.note: a link reopens to the exact decision — matters for non-technical readers
back/forward steps through the decisions you viewed
pure client-side; the single file still works from disk or email
the hash shows the id (#d5), not a human-friendly name

options.label: No linking, default open only (the old way)

tradeoffs.note: can't point someone at one decision; every share lands on the default
needs a server; breaks the offline single-file design
